mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in monthly employment growth signals an approaching economic contraction, prompting equity investors to price in lower future cash flows and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change falls below 50.0 thousand
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the predictive Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between industrial production and housing starts indicates supply chain bottlenecks meeting weak demand, creating margin compression that forces equity multiples lower and yields negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows a positive coefficient in a multivariate regression of 20-day returns, or the joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline by at least 0.02 Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation remains elevated above target while the central bank maintains restrictive policy rates, real interest rates stay positive, suppressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns in this regime are statistically indistinguishable from the unconditional mean (p > 0.10), or the real rate spread fails to predict equity drawdowns.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in consumer credit balances reflects robust aggregate demand, supporting corporate revenue growth expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The rolling 12-month correlation between RSAFS growth and 5-day SPX returns drops below 0.10, or forward returns show no directional bias when credit growth exceeds 1.5% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in new housing permits signals weakening forward-looking demand and construction activity, which reduces corporate earnings guidance and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day window.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1450.0 for two consecutive months
failure_condition: The signal generates positive 20-day forward returns in over 60% of triggered episodes, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for interest rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in total nonfarm payrolls and consumer credit expansion confirms a self-reinforcing demand cycle, elevating risk appetite and equity valuations to produce positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate a positive Sharpe ratio over any 3-year rolling window, or the interaction coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in a factor model.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth outpacing labor market expansion indicates improving productivity and capacity utilization, which lowers unit labor costs and boosts corporate margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: The ratio of INDPRO growth to UNRATE decline shows no correlation with forward equity returns, or the mechanism reverses direction when unemployment falls below 4.0%.
